jokes.text: You ain't no fun to stick up, man.
jokes.setup: Standard armed robbery scenario where victim should be scared
jokes.punchline: Robber complains that the victim isn't entertaining enough during the crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subverts robbery expectations - the criminal is disappointed in his victim's performance, treating it like entertainment. Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Either of us.
jokes.setup: Someone says an accident 'could happen to any of us' trying to comfort John
jokes.punchline: Another character corrects it to 'either of us' - excluding John from the group of safe drivers
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word correction that reveals the speaker doesn't trust John's driving. Great character-based humor with minimal setup.

jokes.text: Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Everyone expresses confidence that Latka can fix the severely damaged cab
jokes.punchline: Latka's non-committal grunt suggests he's not as confident as they are
jokes.explanation: Classic reaction beat - the pause and uncertain response undercuts everyone's confidence. Good use of Latka's character.

jokes.text: I quit.
jokes.setup: Latka confidently says he can fix the cab without a specialist
jokes.punchline: Immediately quits upon seeing the actual damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure - confidence to immediate defeat. The visual of the destroyed cab must be devastating for such a quick reversal.

jokes.text: Don't wait up.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks how late she'll be on a 140-mile trip
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement treating a potentially all-night trip as staying out late
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor - treating a major deviation from work as a minor delay. Shows Elaine's impulsive character.

jokes.text: It's a mug shot.
jokes.setup: Elaine complains about her terrible taxi license photo
jokes.punchline: Mike dryly points out it's literally a mug shot, not meant to be flattering
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor - cuts through Elaine's vanity with basic fact. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: She's the only one who never asked me why I drive a cab.
jokes.setup: Mike asks why Elaine drives a cab, she tells story about difficult passenger
jokes.punchline: The most obnoxious passenger was the only one who didn't pry into her life
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about human nature - sometimes the worst people respect boundaries better than 'nice' ones. Good character insight.

jokes.text: I get them from libraries, you know.
jokes.setup: Elaine talks about renting paintings instead of buying them
jokes.punchline: She gets artwork from libraries like books, treating art as literature
jokes.explanation: Charming character detail showing Elaine's artistic interests constrained by practical limits. Creative solution to wanting art.

jokes.text: I mean, like, I just know it's going to turn out you're either married, gay, or crazy.
jokes.setup: Elaine worries that their connection is too perfect
jokes.punchline: Lists the classic dealbreakers that ruin perfect romantic scenarios
jokes.explanation: Relatable dating anxiety expressed through the classic trio of romantic dealbreakers. Good escalation in the list.

jokes.text: Come on, crazy. Right again. Thank God.
jokes.setup: Elaine guesses Mike is married, gay, or crazy
jokes.punchline: Mike confirms he's crazy, Elaine is relieved it's the 'best' option
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup - crazy is revealed as the most manageable flaw. Elaine's relief is the real punchline.

jokes.text: What is it, a tattoo somewhere that says, 'I have kids'?
jokes.setup: Mike correctly guesses Elaine has children
jokes.punchline: Elaine wonders if parenthood is somehow physically obvious
jokes.explanation: Funny take on how parents think their status might be obvious. The tattoo image is absurd but relatable.

jokes.text: You don't even know when to lie.
jokes.setup: Mike honestly says he doesn't know when he's coming back from London
jokes.punchline: Elaine criticizes his honesty as a character flaw in romantic situations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - honesty presented as incompetence. Captures the complexity of romantic expectations vs. reality.

jokes.text: I'd feel like a call girl. You didn't do anything. I'd feel like a bad call girl.
jokes.setup: Mike offers Elaine $100 tip, she initially refuses
jokes.punchline: Escalates from feeling like a call girl to feeling like an incompetent one
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and self-deprecation. The logic of being a 'bad' call girl for not earning the money is absurdly funny.

jokes.text: What would you have given me if I'd stayed the night? A very sweet memory. You're better off with the hundred.
jokes.setup: Elaine jokingly asks what she'd have gotten for staying
jokes.punchline: Mike's romantic answer followed by his practical assessment
jokes.explanation: Bittersweet humor that's both romantic and pragmatic. Good character moment showing Mike's honesty even in emotional moments.

jokes.text: He did. He took no for an answer.
jokes.setup: Others say the perfect guy must have had a big flaw somewhere
jokes.punchline: His flaw was respecting her boundaries - treating respect as a character defect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - presenting a man's respect for consent as his romantic flaw. Sharp commentary on dating expectations with perfect economy.

jokes.text: I had a woman one time with a head of lettuce she said was alive, and she wanted me to ask it questions.
jokes.setup: Character claims to only have usual passenger stories
jokes.punchline: Describes completely bizarre passenger with living lettuce
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd image treated as mundane. The specificity of asking the lettuce questions makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I lost you when you started taking two at a time.
jokes.setup: Husband was right behind her on stairs
jokes.punchline: She was moving faster despite being pregnant
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy about pregnancy urgency vs. partner panic

jokes.text: With my pelvis, the doctor says I'm gonna drop 'em like kittens.
jokes.setup: Ruth explains she's well-prepared for natural childbirth
jokes.punchline: Overshares intimate medical details using animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic oversharing humor with memorable animal comparison. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: A minute and ten seconds? In phase one? Here comes another one. You just had one. Well, I'm having another one. That's phase three. What happened to phase two?
jokes.setup: Allen is confident about tracking labor phases by the book
jokes.punchline: Biology doesn't follow his textbook schedule, creating panic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as preparation meets reality. Allen's panic about skipping phases is relatable for over-planners.

jokes.text: Me?! Listen, no sweat. I can coach you from here on how to coach her back there.
jokes.setup: Allen faints and Alex has to take over delivering the baby
jokes.punchline: Allen tries to coach from outside while Alex handles the actual emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic irony - the 'expert' becomes useless while coaching from the sidelines. Good character moment for Allen's helplessness.

jokes.text: Coach her, coach her, don't let her coach you. / He's right, he's right. Don't worry about how I am. Make sure she follows you. You're the boss, remember.
jokes.setup: Allen is supposed to be coaching but Alex is doing the work
jokes.punchline: Even Ruth has to remind the actual coach to let Alex take charge
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the role reversal theme. Ruth having to manage the coaching situation while in labor adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: I'm not talking to you. Oh. Push. Are you pushing? I'm pushing!
jokes.setup: Complex three-way communication during labor emergency
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown in who's talking to whom about pushing
jokes.explanation: Situational comedy from communication breakdown under pressure. The confusion feels realistic and panic-driven.

jokes.text: Marry a man who won't even look at our baby?
jokes.setup: Ruth is angry at Allen for not helping during delivery
jokes.punchline: Threatens to call off marriage during peak emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Relationship drama at worst possible timing. Ruth's anger is understandable but poorly timed, creating awkward humor.

jokes.text: Maybe we'll just get you a bottle of scotch.
jokes.setup: Allen asks Alex's name to potentially name their baby after him
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides against it and offers alcohol instead
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax - builds up to naming honor, deflates to standard thank-you gift. Shows Allen's practical limits.

jokes.text: Be careful of steering. You know, sometimes you turn... go this way when the car, it go this way.
jokes.setup: Latka has 'fixed' cab 804 and Alex wants to drive it
jokes.punchline: Steering doesn't work properly - turning one way makes car go another
jokes.explanation: Classic Latka mechanical incompetence. The casual delivery of dangerous information is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Brakes are really good now except when it's wet or very dry.
jokes.setup: Latka continues listing the 'fixed' car's problems
jokes.punchline: Brakes only work in impossible weather conditions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic failure - excludes all normal weather conditions. Latka's cheerful delivery makes the danger funnier.

jokes.text: Be sure don't go over 25 mile per hours because if you do, transmission...
jokes.setup: More warnings about the 'repaired' cab
jokes.punchline: Speed limit to avoid transmission failure - trailing off ominously
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the car's problems. The trailing off implies something catastrophic happens at higher speeds.

jokes.text: On reflection, I think I delivered that baby in 803.
jokes.setup: Alex earned the right to drive 804 first by delivering a baby in it
jokes.punchline: After the car catches fire, he conveniently 'remembers' it was a different cab
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and deflation. Alex's convenient memory revision after the disaster is perfectly timed and character-appropriate.
